title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Mr. Barrington informs Hercule Poirot that the nephew of the estate owner, who is the son of the lady's sister, may inherit the Lichfield-Roches estate and change his surname, as Mr. Lichfield-Roches left no heirs. Poirot then speaks with Mrs. Lichfield-Roches, who attributes the family's troubles to a curse symbolized by a mirror and notes that her husband, Hubert, had become increasingly eccentric.

Poirot questions the butler, Digby, regarding the gunshot. Digby states that Mr. Dahlhaus, Miss Ashby, and Mr. Keene were in the hall at the time, while Mrs. Lichfield-Roches, Miss Clifford, and Mr. Baring arrived later. Poirot then interviews Miss Clifford, who describes the deceased as a man with supreme authority and mental aberrations. She expresses indifference toward his death, noting he was involved in bad investments, though she admits she liked him. She also mentions she was in the garden when the shot occurred.

Next, Poirot confronts Mr. Keene about picking up an object near the study door. Although Keene initially denies it, he eventually admits to possessing a tiny silk rosebud that Poirot identifies as coming from Miss Clifford's purse. 

Finally, Major John Marshall arrives and expresses shock at the suicide. Poirot questions him regarding financial irregularities and his plan to elope with the ward, Daisy. Marshall confirms the relationship, noting that the deceased was unaware of it and that Daisy is handling the situation. Marshall also mentions he is looking for a new job to avoid losing his position if the truth were discovered.
